result.markdown:
--- title: Examples demonstrating Parquet utility functions --- This page groups together examples for the Parquet utility helpers: `pq_list_files()`, `pq_last_modified()`, `pq_archive()`, `pq_restore()`, and `pq_remove()`. The goal is different from the API reference. The API pages document arguments and return values. This page shows how the utilities fit together in the practical workflow of maintaining a local Parquet repository. ## When to use this page Use these helpers when you already have a Parquet repository and want to: - inspect what is in it - check which vintage of a table you currently have - archive a current active file before replacing it - restore an older archived vintage - remove active or archived files explicitly The related API pages are: - [files.paths.pq_list_files](reference/files.paths.pq_list_files.qmd) - [files.parquet.pq_last_modified](reference/files.parquet.pq_last_modified.qmd) - [files.parquet.pq_archive](reference/files.parquet.pq_archive.qmd) - [files.parquet.pq_restore](reference/files.parquet.pq_restore.qmd) - [files.parquet.pq_remove](reference/files.parquet.pq_remove.qmd) ## Setup The executable examples below assume access to a local Parquet repository with at least one known schema and table. Adjust these values to match your local setup before rendering if needed. ## Inspect a schema directory Start by listing the Parquet tables available in a schema. ::: {#cell-parquet-utils-list-schema .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} pq_list_files("comp") ``` ::: {#parquet-utils-list-schema .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=17} ``` ['r_auditors', 'aco_pnfnda', 'fundq', 'funda', 'idx_daily', 'funda_fncd', 'company'] ``` ::: ::: If you want to inspect the archive directory instead: ::: {#cell-parquet-utils-list-archive .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} pq_list_files("comp", archive=True) ``` ::: {#parquet-utils-list-archive .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=18} ``` ['funda_20260406T060000Z'] ``` ::: ::: If your project uses a repository outside the default `DATA_DIR`, pass `data_dir` explicitly. ::: {#cell-parquet-utils-list-explicit-dir .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} pq_list_files("ff", data_dir="~/Dropbox/pq_data") ``` ::: {#parquet-utils-list-explicit-dir .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=19} ``` ['factors_daily', 'industry48', 'factors_monthly'] ``` ::: ::: ## Check the current active vintage Use `pq_last_modified()` to inspect the embedded metadata on the active file. ::: {#cell-parquet-utils-active-vintage .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} pq_last_modified(table_name="dsf", schema="crsp") ``` ::: {#parquet-utils-active-vintage .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=20} ``` 'Daily Stock - Securities (Updated 2025-02-08)' ``` ::: ::: This is often the fastest way to confirm what vintage a local Parquet file represents before starting analysis. You can also inspect a specific file path directly: ::: {#cell-parquet-utils-explicit-file .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} dsf_v2 = Path.home() / "Dropbox/pq_data/crsp/dsf_v2.parquet" pq_last_modified(file_name=dsf_v2) ``` ::: {#parquet-utils-explicit-file .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=21} ``` 'Daily Stock File created by WRDS (Updated 2026-02-06)' ``` ::: ::: ## Inspect archived vintages If you archive replaced files, you can ask for the archived versions of a table: ::: {#cell-parquet-utils-archived-vintages .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} pq_last_modified(table_name="funda", schema="comp", archive=True) ``` ::: {#parquet-utils-archived-vintages .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=22} ```{=html}
file_name table schema last_mod last_mod_str storage
0 funda_20260406T060000Z funda comp 2026-04-06 02:00:00-04:00 Merged Fundamental Annual File (Updated 2026-0... local
``` ::: ::: That returns a table-like summary of the archived vintages for the requested dataset. To inspect all archived files for a schema: ::: {#cell-parquet-utils-schema-archive-summary .cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} pq_last_modified(schema="comp", archive=True) ``` ::: {#parquet-utils-schema-archive-summary .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=23} ```{=html}
file_name table schema last_mod last_mod_str storage
0 funda_20260406T060000Z funda comp 2026-04-06 02:00:00-04:00 Merged Fundamental Annual File (Updated 2026-0... local
``` ::: ::: ## Archive the currently active file You can archive a file manually even outside an update workflow. ::: {#4b9e8d22 .cell execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code} from db2pq import pq_archive pq_archive(table_name="funda", schema="comp") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=24} ``` '/Users/igow/Dropbox/far_data/comp/archive/funda_20260331T060000Z.parquet' ``` ::: ::: Or archive an exact file path: ::: {#72d3e13a .cell execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code} funda = Path.home() / "Dropbox/pq_data/comp/funda.parquet" funda_archive = pq_archive(file_name=funda) funda_archive ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=25} ``` '/Users/igow/Dropbox/pq_data/comp/archive/funda_20260331T060000Z.parquet' ``` ::: ::: This is useful when you want to preserve the current active vintage before running an experimental refresh or downstream transformation. ## Restore an archived vintage To promote an archived file back into the active schema directory: ::: {#57b63cae .cell execution_count=12} ``` {.python .cell-code} from db2pq import pq_restore archive_files = pq_list_files("comp", archive=True) if archive_files: pq_restore(archive_files[0], "comp") ``` ::: The archived basename may include or omit the `.parquet` suffix. If an active destination file already exists, `pq_restore()` archives that file first by default before restoring the archived vintage. ## Remove a file explicitly Use `pq_remove()` when you want to delete an active or archived file rather than archive it. ::: {#3b7da6da .cell execution_count=13} ``` {.python .cell-code} from db2pq import pq_remove pq_remove(table_name="dsi", schema="crsp") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=28} ``` '/Users/igow/Dropbox/far_data/crsp/dsi.parquet' ``` ::: ::: Of course, I probably want that file, so let me use `wrds_update_pq()` to recover it! ::: {#43a2ba5b .cell execution_count=14} ``` {.python .cell-code} from db2pq import wrds_update_pq wrds_update_pq(table_name="dsi", schema="crsp") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Updated crsp.dsi is available. Beginning file download at 2026-04-06 15:58:05 UTC. Completed file download at 2026-04-06 15:58:08 UTC. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=29} ``` '/Users/igow/Dropbox/far_data/crsp/dsi.parquet' ``` ::: ::: To remove an archived file: ::: {#499d05a0 .cell execution_count=15} ``` {.python .cell-code} pq_remove( table_name="funda_20260331T060000Z", schema="comp", archive=True, ) ``` ::: Or remove a file by exact path: ::: {#ce487a7a .cell execution_count=16} ``` {.python .cell-code} funda_archive ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=30} ``` '/Users/igow/Dropbox/pq_data/comp/archive/funda_20260331T060000Z.parquet' ``` ::: ::: ::: {#55a27543 .cell execution_count=17} ``` {.python .cell-code} pq_remove(file_name=funda_archive) ``` ::: ## Related pages - [Data management ideas](data-management.qmd) - [WRDS to Parquet](wrds-to-pq.qmd) - [PostgreSQL to Parquet](pg-to-pq.qmd) - [API Reference](reference/index.qmd)
